attributes.description: Alerts when average CPU utilization of reserved task CPU exceeds a threshold. Sustained high CPU throttles workloads and raises application latency.
attributes.name: [AWS ECS OTel] CPU utilization high

investigation_guide.blob:
## CPU Utilization High

`CPUUtilization` is the percentage of reserved CPU in use. Fargate throttles CPU at the reservation ceiling, which manifests as rising latency at the ALB/application layer.

### Triage Steps
1. Correlate with ALB `TargetResponseTime` and application latency metrics.
2. Check whether traffic increased without a corresponding task count scale-up.
3. Review task definition CPU reservation — undersized reservations pin near 100%.
4. Inspect CPU profiles or hot code paths in the container workload.
5. Compare with `CpuUtilized` / `CpuReserved` from Container Insights for absolute CPU units.

### Customisation
- Default threshold is 85% of reserved CPU — tune per service SLO and headroom needs.
- Duplicate with `stat == "Maximum"` to catch short CPU spikes.

esqlQuery.esql:
FROM metrics-aws.ecs.otel-*
| WHERE Namespace == "AWS/ECS"
    AND MetricName == "CPUUtilization"
    AND stat == "Average"
| STATS avg_cpu_util = AVG(`metrics.amazonaws.com/AWS/ECS/CPUUtilization`)
    BY ClusterName, ServiceName, cloud.region
// Percent of reserved CPU — tune threshold (default: 85%)
| WHERE avg_cpu_util > 85.0
| EVAL ecs_service = CONCAT(ClusterName, "/", ServiceName, " (", cloud.region, ")")
| SORT avg_cpu_util DESC
